other_observers: Nick Komar, Nick A. Komar, and Steve Messick; Steve Messick originally found the bird and came and got me and the other observers with me. Rachel Hopper saw it the morning of May 21.
specific_location: About 1 mile east of County Road 57 on County Road 100. About 3 miles north of Highway 14.
description: It had an orange face with black auriculars and eye stripe. The throat was the brightest of the orange on the head. It had a black crown aroung an orange stripe and a few orange spots on top of the head. It had yellow lower breast and upper belly. Black stripes on the sides and flanks. It had a black and white back. White lower belly and undertail covert.Behaviors: It was usually half way up one of three different Cottonwood Trees. It was in the same set of trees both days that I saw the bird.Call: High pitched song: Tsi tsi tsi tsi tsi tsiiiii.Plumage: breeding adult
